LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JAMES CHIN, ORHAN TOLU, AND, HILFASCO INCORPORATED NOTICE OF MOTION FOR FEES. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR FEES DENIED. THE UNDERLYING SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT WAS RAISED AS A DEFENSE TO THE COMPLAINT AND WAS NOT A BASIS ON WHICH PLAINTIFF SOUGHT RELIEF. NO PARTY BROUGHT AN ACTION AGAINST THE OTHER BY REASON OF ANY BREACH OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. THE REAL ESTATE PURCHASE AGREEMENT WAS NOT THE BASIS ON WHICH PLAINTIFF SOUGHT RELIEF. RATHER, THE VARIOUS FRAUD, BREACH OF CONTRACT, ELDER ABUSE AND MISREPRESENTATION ARGUMENTS RELATED TO AN ALLEGED ORAL "AGENCY AGREEMENT" WITHOUT AN ATTORNEY FEE PROVISION. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS GRANTED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS HILFASCO, INC, JAMES W. CHIN, AND ORHAN TOLU'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF MAPLE RABB'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF KNEW THE TRUTH OF THE ALLEGED FRAUD BEFORE CLOSING ESCROW WHICH DEFEATS THE FRAUD, MISREPRESENTATION AND CONCEALMENT CLAIMS. THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO STATE A CLAIM AND IS AMBIGUOUS. NO PROPERTY WAS TAKEN TO SUPPORT THE ELDER ABUSE CAUSE OF ACTION. THERE IS NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT PLAINTIFF CAN AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Fees
Off Calendar Sep-21-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JAMES CHIN, ORHAN TOLU, and, HILFASCO INCORPORATED Notice of Motion for Fees. Appearances: Vernon C. Goins II, Taylor, Goins & Stallworth LLP (510) 893-9465, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Jason Granskog, of Bowles Verna LLP (925) 935-3300 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' Motion for fees denied. The underlying settlement agreement was raised as a defense to the complaint and was not a basis on which plaintiff sought relief. No party brought an action against the other by reason of any breach of the settlement agreement. The Real Estate Purchase Agreement was not the basis on which plaintiff sought relief. Rather, the various fraud, breach of contract, elder abuse and misrepresentation arguments related to an alleged oral "Agency Agreement" without an attorney fee provision. Plaintiff's motion to tax costs granted. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Notice Of Demurrer To Pltf Maple Rabb'S Second Amended Complaint; Demurrer; Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Demurrer To Pltf
Off Calendar May-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS HILFASCO, INC. dba CENTURY 21 ALLIANCE, JAMES W. CHIN, AND ORHAN TOLU'S Demurrer To Plaintiff Maple Rabb's Second Amended Complaint.
APPEARANCES: Vernon C. Goins, II, Esq. of Taylor, Goins & Stallworth LLP 510/893-9465 for pltf & opposing party; Steven P. McFarlane, Esq. of Bowles & Verna LLP 925/935-3300 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling and adopted as follows: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF KNEW THE TRUTH OF THE ALLEGED FRAUD BEFORE CLOSING ESCROW WHICH DEFEATS THE FRAUD, MISREPRESENTATION AND CONCEALMENT CLAIMS. THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO STATE A CLAIM AND IS AMBIGUOUS. NO PROPERTY WAS TAKEN TO SUPPORT THE ELDER ABUSE CAUSE OF ACTION. THERE IS NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT PLAINTIFF CAN AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. Defendant to prepare proposed form of order. (302/CWW/JW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
